dataset.note: 154 sidecars (jupiter system + moon); tour-moon frames blocked on targeted CK download (P2)

blockers: G0 fails: mean|axis err| 0.165% (need <=0.05%), p99 1.30% (need <=0.2%). Root cause identified: moderate/high-phase (alpha>=60 deg) Lambert-crescent limb localization carries a systematic 0.3-2% bias. See DECK.md decision log for the full mechanism (pixel-aperture smoothing of the Lambert sqrt-profile's slope singularity, not reproducible by a pure-Gaussian 1D-radial blur model) and what was tried.
next_actions: P1 (blocking G0): design a limb-profile model that accounts for pixel-aperture (or 2D tangential) blur near the Lambert sqrt kink -- algorithm-architect task; see DECK.md for the diagnostic trail (diag_physics/diag_box scratch tools) so it isn't re-derived from scratch

P1: once G0 passes, wire the ONNX detection gate (cv::dnn) + Sun-hint plumbing into measure_limb/batch_eval for real-frame runs (today's real-Rhea-frame smoke test returned a nonsensical a_px=2193 on a 1024px image -- expected given no ROI seed / no sun hint yet, not a regression)
P1: validate sidecar pixel convention against real frames (clear PIXEL_CONVENTION_UNVALIDATED)
P2 prep: targeted CK downloads for Saturn-tour moon frames (per-image kernel selection)
P4 prep: librarian sweeps per research/literature/SEARCH_LOG.md
